questions: The standard layout has `gap-3 p-3` — is gap-3 applied between all grid cells (so there's a 12px gap between the preview column and the 360px properties column)? If padding or gap values differ from what I read in the template literals, the PROPERTIES_AND_GAPS constant would need adjustment. This is low-risk since I can verify from the code.

Should there be hysteresis (e.g., switch to condensed at threshold X but switch back at X + 20px) to prevent flickering at the boundary? The current implementation has no hysteresis, so matching that behavior seems fine, but worth flagging.

success_criteria.criterion: Condensed mode threshold accounts for video aspect ratio: portrait videos (e.g. 9:16) trigger condensed at a higher window height than landscape (16:9)
When resolvedConfig is not yet loaded, falls back to 16:9 aspect ratio (no crash, no NaN)
MIN_PREVIEW_HEIGHT (180px) remains a floor — threshold never drops below the original minimum for very wide windows
ResizeObserver still fires on width changes (not just height), so narrowing the window also updates the threshold dynamically
No flickering/oscillation at the condensed mode boundary during normal resize
PROPERTIES_AND_GAPS constant matches actual layout spacing from the grid template
Change is contained to VideoEditorShell.tsx (single file, ~15 lines)

assumptions: gap-3 = 12px gap between grid cells, p-3 = 12px padding on each side — so horizontal non-preview space is 360 + 12 + 24 = 396px. Will verify from Tailwind defaults.
The chrome overhead (40 + 28 + 24 = 92px for header + divider + padding) is correct as-is and doesn't need aspect-ratio adjustment.
No hysteresis needed — matching current behavior where the ResizeObserver directly compares height to threshold.
16:9 is a safe fallback aspect ratio when resolvedConfig is not yet loaded.
The `resolution` field format is always 'WIDTHxHEIGHT' (e.g. '1920x1080') — parseResolution handles this.
